do_not_copy: Do not copy source visuals, layouts, screenshots, brand marks, article prose, example slides, or tutorial assets.
observed_move: Move proof detail out of the main story so the primary deck can keep rhythm while still preserving auditability.
why_it_works: Hierarchy is partly an information-routing decision. Appendix material can protect credibility without forcing the main audience through every supporting detail.
ppt_translation: Route dense evidence into editable appendix slides, speaker notes, or linked native PowerPoint tables while keeping the six-slide main path low-density.
quality_risk: The deck becomes a report if too much appendix detail remains on main slides or if every proof note is promoted to visible body text.

extraction_units.source_anchor: tutorial guidance on hierarchy and supporting detail
extraction_units.derived_rule: Route dense proof detail out of the main six-slide path before reducing font size.
extraction_units.execution_guard: Do not compress audit detail into tiny visible labels on main slides.
extraction_units.qa_probe: Main slides keep one dominant claim while detailed evidence remains findable in notes or appendix.

extraction_units.source_anchor: tutorial distinction between primary message and support material
extraction_units.derived_rule: Preserve evidence as editable notes, appendix tables, or result reports instead of deleting it silently.
extraction_units.execution_guard: Do not remove proof that is required for the release gate.
extraction_units.qa_probe: Trace notes identify what was routed away from each crowded slide.
